manageClusterDesc: Initialize and manage your MaxIOFS cluster
monitorClusterDesc: Monitor and manage your MaxIOFS cluster
clusterNotInitializedDesc: Initialize a new cluster or join an existing one to enable multi-node replication and high availability
initClusterDesc: This will create a new cluster with this node as the first member. You will receive a cluster token to add other nodes.
nodeAddressHint: The IP address other cluster nodes will use to reach this server (e.g. 192.168.1.10, 10.0.0.5).
joinClusterDesc: Enter the endpoint of an existing cluster node and the cluster token to join.
clusterNodeEndpointHint: IP address (or IP:port) of an existing cluster node. Default port is 8082.
clusterTokenHint: The token displayed when the cluster was initialized
saveTokenWarning: Save this token now. You will need it to join other nodes to this cluster.
clusterTokenDesc: Use this token to join other nodes to this cluster. You can provide it in the "Join Existing Cluster" form on a standalone node, or use "Add Node" from this cluster to add nodes automatically.
confirmRemoveNode: Are you sure you want to remove this node from the cluster?
syncIntervalMin10: Sync interval must be at least 10 seconds
manageNodesDesc: Manage nodes in your MaxIOFS cluster
noNodesYet: No nodes added yet. Add your first node to start building the cluster.
addNodeDesc: Enter the console URL and admin credentials of the remote node. The node must be in standalone mode (not already in a cluster).
consoleUrlHint: IP address of the remote node (console port 8081). Cluster communication will use port 8082 automatically.
endpointCannotChange: Endpoint cannot be changed. Remove and re-add the node to change the endpoint.
priorityHint: Lower values = higher priority for routing (1-1000)
bulkReplicationDesc: This will configure cluster replication for all local buckets to the selected target node. Nodes authenticate using cluster tokens - no credentials needed.
allBucketsReplicatedHint: All local buckets will be replicated to this node. Note: Local node is not shown (cannot replicate to itself).
syncIntervalHint: Minimum 10 seconds. Use 60 for real-time HA, or higher values for backups.
clusterBucketReplicationDesc: Configure high-availability replication between cluster nodes
noBucketsMatchingFilter: No buckets found matching the filter
clusterReplicationInfoDesc: Nodes authenticate using cluster tokens - no credentials needed. Objects are automatically encrypted/decrypted during replication.
localNodeNotShownHint: Note: Local node is not shown. Cluster replication is for HA between different MaxIOFS servers.
someNodesUnhealthy: ⚠️ Some nodes are unhealthy. Replication may be affected.
syncIntervalHintLong: Minimum 10 seconds. Use 60 for real-time HA, or higher values (e.g., 21600 = 6 hours) for backups.
prefixFilterHint: Only replicate objects with keys starting with this prefix
bucketMigrationsDesc: View migration history and manage active migrations
onlyHealthyNodesExcluding: Only healthy nodes (excluding source node {{id}}) are shown
deleteSourceData: Delete source data after successful migration
migrationWarning: Warning: Migration will move all bucket data to the target node. Ensure the target node has sufficient storage space.
selectBucketAndNode: Please select a bucket and target node
sameNodeError: Source node and target node cannot be the same!
haReplicationDesc: Cluster-wide replication factor — applies to all buckets on all nodes
haLoadError: Failed to load cluster HA status. Is the cluster enabled?
storagePressureDesc_one: {{nodes}} is above 80% capacity. Consider expanding storage or reducing the replication factor.
storagePressureDesc_other: {{nodes}} are above 80% capacity. Consider expanding storage or reducing the replication factor.
confirmDowngradeBody: You are reducing the replication factor from ×{{from}} to ×{{to}}. {{desc}} This will remove redundant copies from some nodes in the background.
confirmChangeBody: You are changing the replication factor to ×{{factor}}. {{desc}} The cluster will sync all data to new replicas in the background.
factorNoReplicationDesc: Single copy — no redundancy. Cluster fails if any node goes down.
factorMirrorDesc: Two complete copies. Tolerates 1 simultaneous node failure.
factorTripleDesc: Three complete copies. Tolerates 2 simultaneous node failures.
replicaSyncDesc: Initial and catch-up sync jobs for each replica node
